thesis: Pre-revenue solid-state battery developer with a proprietary ceramic separator technology and anchor partnership with Volkswagen. Eagle Line pilot production is now operational, but commercial revenue is unlikely before 2027–2028. Binary outcome: successful licensing model or cash-burn-to-zero. Elevated short interest (17%) creates squeeze potential on positive OEM news.

drivers: Eagle Line produces validation cells that meet OEM specs
Volkswagen and 2+ additional OEMs sign licensing by 2027
Solid-state batteries gain regulatory preference due to safety
First commercial revenue in 2027–2028, accelerating through 2030

risks: Technical setbacks scaling the ceramic separator
Competition (Toyota, Samsung SDI) reaches market first
Distressed capital raise heavily dilutes shareholders

base_case.assumptions: Technology works but timeline extends to 2029–2030; single major OEM partner; modest licensing revenue by 2030; one or more dilutive capital raises before profitability.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — Eagle Line operational update
Potential first sample cell deliveries to OEMs for validation

key_risks: Pre-revenue with ~2.5–3 years of cash runway
Intensifying competition from Toyota, Samsung SDI, Solid Power
High dilution risk from future capital raises
EV demand slowdown reduces OEM urgency for next-gen batteries
